observations.managementAction: Submission of CY 2019 GPB within the prescribed reglementary period.
observations.reason: Failure to establish a functional GAD database, capacitate GFPS thru attendance to GAD mandatory trainings and establish as Municipal GAD Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: SB revisit the provisions embodied in the 1994 Revenue Code relative to Market fee imposition and policies and procedures on Market operations and make pertinent revisions/amendments thereto, if warranted; Management ensure that the use and occupation on the Market stall units are covered with Contract of Lease and MOA for the collection of the stallholders’ counterpart for the facility improvements made by the Municipality to ensure that the terms and conditions agreed upon by both parties will be complied and their interests will be safeguarded; Designated Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer coordinate to enforce immediate & full collection of rental fees and corresponding surcharges from the delinquent stallholders; Municipal Mayor make formal demand to Ms. Rosita Ramos and Ms. Nenita Puri to restitute the demolished partition walls, otherwise, file appropriate charges against them to indemnify damages sustained by the public market from the act of the stallholders; Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit Monthly Report of Collection to the Accounting Office for proper performance monitoring and recording of receipts; Municipal Accountant prepare separate financial statements to account and monitor the operations of the Public Market and thereafter establish the appropriate receivable account/s to recognize the amount of accumulated uncollected stall rentals and unpaid counterpart dues for the installation of roll-up doors and disclose the nature of the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: Restitution of the demolished intervening walls between stalls of same lessee.
observations.reason: Action has yet to be taken to address the issuance of the Contract of Lease between the LGU and the stall holders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer (a) submit justification/explanation for the failure to remit the taxes withheld under SEF and TF, and (b) continue withholding and remitting the monthly contributions and other amounts due within the prescribed period and analyze the balances for each account and make the necessary remittance or adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: Partial remittance of BIR withholding for TF and SEF.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliati on and analysis of records.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our recommendation that the: Municipal Mayor create and establish an Inventory and Appraisal Committee, headed by the GSO with representatives from the Accounting, Engineering and Assessor’s Office, which will prepare a plan for the conduct of a complete physical inventory including the time frame to complete inventory count and the preparation of the report, taking into consideration the present condition and location of the properties. Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) must be made available for fast tracing of the properties and should be updated to establish proper accountability; OIC-GSO obtain from the Accounting Office the listing of properties per category of PPE as reference in the inventory taking; Thereafter, Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO coordinate and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the proper reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties; Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain RPI for all Public Infrastructure Assets, trace back records of the completed Infrastructure Assets starting from the current year backwards and prepare separate Subsidiary Ledgers for the unreconciled account balances and observe proper disclosure of the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: Partial remittance and ongoing actions.
observations.reason: Lack of reliable data and personnel to maintain required records and conduct the complete inventory-taking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: MPDC coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of developmental projects to optimally utilize 20% DF. Moreover, assign qualified personnel who can focus on the monitoring of implementation of PPAs and require the regular submission of status report which will aide in timely implementation of various PPAs of the Municipality; Officials and employees concerned re-visit the policy on award and implementation of contract as prescribed in the RIRR of RA No. 9184; BAC, Municipal Engineer and GSO provide reasonable explanation for the noted deficiencies in contract and actual project implementation; Municipal Engineer and Accountant evaluate the delay incurred in project completion of certain projects as presented in Annex “K” and compute for any liquidated damages that should be demanded from the contractor, if applicable; BAC Secretariat, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents within five (5) days upon receipt hereof to support the propriety and validity of the disbursements made to avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the subjected transactions; Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required; and BAC and Municipal Accountant require the posting of warranty securities for infrastructure projects in accordance with the prescribed rate under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management on several items; some projects were realigned and supplemental budgets approved for certain priorities.
observations.reason: Change of priority projects/program; delayed implementati on of procurement schedule; unsuccessful procurement activities resulting to prolonged procurement process; insufficiency of funds; projects created on the later part of the year; projects that are still on the preparatory process of project implementati on and lack of concrete plan for the implementati on of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) exert effort to determine the validity of the amount in the accounting records and adjust as necessary; (b) secure copies of bank debit and credit memos so that necessary adjustments in the GF, SEF, and TF books can be made; (c) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to trace variances and reconcile balances on a regular basis; and (d) prepare JEVs to adjust stale checks, copy furnish the Treasurer to effect proper adjustment in the cashbooks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to exert effort to determine the validity of the reconciling items and prepare the necessary adjustments; copies of some bank memos were requested; coordination with Treasurer to reconcile balances was initiated.
observations.reason: Management has not taken full action to reconcile balances and adjust reconciling items; outstanding reconciling items and stale checks remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality expedite the settlement and resolution of unsettled suspensions, disallowances and charges and coordinate with COA and concerned offices to address issues.
observations.managementAction: No specific management action recorded in the table; follow-up actions to settle suspensions and disallowances ongoing.
observations.reason: Unsettled suspensions and disallowances remain outstanding as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: ➢ Municipal Assessor, OIC-GSO and OIC-Treasurer coordinate to obtain the necessary documents for titling and registration of parcels; and ➢ OIC-Budget Officer annually appropriate funds on a per parcel basis to ensure that the Municipality’s budget can accommodate the survey and titling of each land in the name of the Municipality to preserve legal rights over the real properties and to avoid exposure to third party claims.
observations.managementAction: Management has not taken action yet to secure necessary documents; some coordination efforts planned.
observations.reason: No action taken; missing documentation and resources to effect titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to analyze, confirm and ascertain the validity of the long outstanding liabilities in the Trust Fund book and if found with valid claims notify the claimants, for other payables account, if found without valid documentation and/or which have been outstanding for more than two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed, proper adjustment in the books of accounts can be made and return the amount to the source agencies or to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing verification of the Trust Fund items; Management to follow up claimants and prepare adjustments or remittances as appropriate.
observations.reason: Verification pending due to unavailability of records and supporting documents; actions to revert or remit amounts not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our recommendation that the: ➢ Municipal Mayor create and establish an Inventory and Appraisal Committee, headed by the GSO with representatives from the Accounting, Engineering and Assessor’s Office, which will prepare a plan for the conduct of a complete physical inventory including the time frame to complete inventory count and the preparation of the report, taking into consideration the present condition and location of the properties. Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) must be made available for fast tracing of the properties and should be updated to establish proper accountability; ➢ OIC-GSO obtain from the Accounting Office the listing of properties per category of PPE as reference in the inventory taking; Thereafter, ➢ Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO coordinate and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the proper reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties; and ➢ Municipal Accountant maintain property ledger cards that will provide a complete breakdown of the amounts reflected in the financial statements, effect proper adjustments in the books for items that necessitate corrections as identified in the verification and reconciliation process, create separate subsidiary ledgers for the unreconciled account balances, and observe proper disclosure of the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: The GSO- Designate was able to prepare an inventory report based on the previous property records indicating therein properties totaling ₱142,977,990.58, of which ₱70,914,615.65 represents unidentified beginning balances which was excluded from the reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: Inability to perform an inventory count due to failure to create an Inventory and Appraisal Committee and non-maintenance of property cards; and non-preparation of RPCLRN.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the MPDO with the assistance of the MPDO reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented PPAs of prior years, otherwise reprogram the same to more needed developmental projects. The Council should also properly plan, including specific identification of projects to be implemented and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year; MBO and MDC ensure that sufficient fund will be appropriated for the complete implementation of the identified projects; Officials and employees concerned re-visit the policy on award and implementation of contract as prescribed in the RIRR of RA No. 9184; BAC, Municipal Engineer and GSO provide reasonable explanation for the noted deficiencies in contract and actual project implementation; Municipal Engineer and Accountant evaluate the delay incurred in project completion as presented in Annex “K” and compute for any liquidated damages; BAC Secretariat, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents within five (5) days upon receipt hereof to support the propriety and validity of the disbursements made to avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the subjected transactions; Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445, Annex C of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and RA 9184 and its Revised IRR; and BAC and Municipal Accountant require the posting of warranty securities for infrastructure projects in accordance with the prescribed rate under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Development Council and MBO submitted justification for the appropriation and inclusion of PPAs not in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1. Moreover, the projects under question were realigned as appropriate.
observations.reason: There were PPAs under 20% DF which are not yet implemented during the year; some projects were realigned and some funds reverted to unappropriated surplus.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: ➢ MPDC coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of developmental projects to optimally utilize 20% DF; assign qualified personnel who can focus on the monitoring of the implementation of various PPAs and require the regular submission of status report which will aide in timely implementation of various PPAs of the Municipality; ➢ MDC and Budget Officer appropriate sufficient fund for the complete implementation of the identified projects and re-appropriate the unexpended balance of 20% DF to other developmental projects.
observations.managementAction: Moreover, through the efforts of concerned officials and employees, some PPAs were implemented and savings from implemented projects were realigned; the Municipality reverted ₱402,090.43 into unappropriated surplus and subjected the same for re-appropriation.
observations.reason: There were unimplemented PPAs due to lack of prioritization and insufficient funds; some savings were reverted and subject to re-appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials and employees: a. Submit satisfactory explanation relative to the noted observations in the grant of financial assistance; b. Regulate the grant of financial assistance to the most essential service only and ensure that funds disbursed are properly utilized for public welfare; c. Ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation and exercise the prudence as required by the service; and d. Accomplish the General Intake Sheet properly by filling up the appropriate sections of the GIS.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials and employees submitted explanations and some refunds (e.g., refund of unliquidated CA) were made; the Municipal Mayor and implementing offices agreed to revise and enforce guidelines.
observations.reason: There were reiterated recommendations and partial refunds made; weaknesses remain in monitoring and documentation of grants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: ➢ Individual Accountable Officers (AOs) strictly observe diligence in the liquidation of their respective CAs within the prescribed period, return excess CAs simultaneously with the liquidation report, and in case of cancelled travel, immediately and fully refund the cash advance and submit valid justification for not pursuing the intended travel; ➢ Municipal Accountant monitor the liquidation of CAs and issue demand letters to concerned Officials and Employees with CAs that remained unsettled for more than thirty (30) days; and ➢ Municipal Treasurer and Accountant withhold the salaries of officials and employees concerned with overdue unliquidated CAs until full liquidation/settlement has been rendered by the AOs, and prohibit the application of salary deduction as means of settlement for their accountabilities, to prevent misappropriation of public funds.
observations.managementAction: The SB Secretary refunded the unliquidated CA amounting to ₱18,870.00 under OR No. 0506678 dated June 10, 2019. The Municipal Accountant promised to monitor the CAs granted and create an internal policy for withholding of salaries for those who fail to immediately refund CAs. Management agreed to enforce recommendations.
observations.reason: Weak internal control on grant and liquidation of CAs persists; some recoveries and actions taken but outstanding unliquidated CAs and procedural lapses remain.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the: ➢ Sangguniang Bayan revisit the provisions embodied in the 1994 Revenue Code relative to Market fee imposition and policies and procedures on Market operations and make pertinent revisions/amendments thereto, if warranted; ➢ Management ensure that the use and occupation on the Market stall units are covered with Contract of Lease and MOA for the collection of the stallholders’ counterpart for the facility improvements made by the Municipality to ensure that the terms and conditions agreed upon by both parties will be complied and their interests will be safeguarded; devise plans and strategies to facilitate effective management of Market operations which may include: (i) creation of a Market Development Plan for the proper organization and classification of market sections; (ii) imposition of additional stall fees to lessees who continuously occupy excess spaces; (iii) issuance of demand letters to stallholders to encourage the timely payment of rental dues and avoidance of penalties; and (iv) issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Eviction to lessees who habitually and/or continuously violate the regulations set forth by the Revenue Code; ➢ Designated Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer coordinate to enforce immediate & full collection of rental fees and corresponding surcharges from the delinquent stallholders; and ➢ Municipal Accountant prepare separate Financial Statements to account for and monitor the operations of the Public Market and thereafter establish the appropriate receivable account/s to recognize the amount of accumulated uncollected stall rentals and unpaid counterpart dues for the installation of roll-up doors and disclose the nature of the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: Through the efforts of the MTO, outstanding collectibles decreased from ₱1.2M during CY 2017 to ₱390,000.00 as of year-end. Management plans to create TWG to facilitate amendment of Revenue Code and to execute contracts of lease for registered occupants.
observations.reason: Continuous failure of the Sangguniang Bayan to amend Revenue Code; lack of binding lease contracts and full enforcement; collections improved but issues persist.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the Municipality in educating their respective constituents through continuous IEC on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and the importance of their participation in the implementation of various SWM programs; (b) require the MENRO-Designate to (i) implement the minimum requirements in operating the existing dumpsite particularly the maintenance of basic record keeping, (ii) coordinate for provision of security fencing to prevent illegal entries, (iii) coordinate for training of operator/s for the technical operation of the composting facility and secure signed MOA, and (iv) ensure that the shredder was not exposed to further deterioration; and (c) require the officials and employees concerned to prioritize and expedite the construction of RCA in order to comply with the requirements of DENR-EMB MIMAROPA for the approval of the SCRP and its subsequent implementation.
observations.managementAction: Through the efforts of the MENRO-Designate, the Municipality’s Ten-Year SWM Plan was approved by NSWMC and all 13 barangays have established MRFs. Management noted action points for RCA establishment and operationalization of composting facility.
observations.reason: Despite approval of SWM plan and establishment of MRFs, failure to comply with basic dumpsite operating requirements due to lack of manpower and prioritization; RCA not yet constructed; composting facility not operational.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and concerned offices submit the lacking supporting documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursements and ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 and RA 9184 and its IRR. Also, ensure posting of necessary documents and observance of procurement timelines; impose liquidated damages where applicable.
observations.managementAction: Various explanations and some supporting documents were submitted by implementing offices; some refunds and adjustments were effected where appropriate; BAC and TWG to submit remaining documents.
observations.reason: Non-submission of all required supporting documents; some procurements completed but documentation gaps remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the balances of the items under the Due to Other NGAs account totaling ₱968,951.40 and subsequently prepare the DV for the return of the unutilized balances and the Municipal Treasurer remit the same to the source agency or to the Bureau of Treasury (BTr). Moreover, conduct regular monitoring and review of the Due to NGAs account and remit any unutilized amount to the SA or BTr upon completion of the project or activity for which the fund is utilized.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that they will analyze the balances and eventually remit the unutilized amount to the source agency or to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: Analysis initiated but remittance not yet completed; verification pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the MPDO, Municipal Development Council (MDC), and MBO submit justification for the appropriation and inclusion of PPAs not in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1. Moreover, make the necessary reprogramming of the unutilized appropriation to suitable developmental projects, including those programs which do not provide specific and clear purposes as prescribed in the JMC.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO, MDC and MBO submitted explanations and justifications; some projects were realigned/reprogrammed and appropriate action taken.
observations.reason: Some justifications and realignments made; some appropriations still not in full conformity and subject to reprogramming.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant reconcile their records at the end of each month as prescribed in Manual on the NGAS for LGU, Volume I for proper, accurate and reliable reporting of the actual efficiency and effectiveness of the rate of utilization of the fund of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Accounting and Budget Offices were instructed to reconcile records; explanation given that differences arise from timing of recording (PR vs actual disbursement).
observations.reason: Differences remain due to different recording practices between Budget and Accounting Offices; reconciliation to be institutionalized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the: (a) MDC with the assistance of the MPDO reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented PPAs of prior years, otherwise reprogram the same to more needed developmental projects. The Council should also properly plan, including specific identification of projects to be implemented and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year; (b) MBO and MDC ensure that sufficient fund will be appropriated for the complete implementation of the identified projects; (c) Officials and employees concerned re-visit the policy on award and implementation of contract as prescribed in the RIRR of RA No. 9184; (d) BAC, Municipal Engineer and GSO provide reasonable explanation for the noted deficiencies in contract and actual project implementation; (e) Municipal Engineer and Accountant evaluate the delay incurred in project completion of certain projects as presented in Annex “K” and compute for any liquidated damages; (f) BAC Secretariat, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents within five (5) days upon receipt hereof to support the propriety and validity of the disbursements made to avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the subjected transactions; (g) Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445, Annex C of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and RA 9184 and its Revised IRR; and (h) BAC and Municipal Accountant require the posting of warranty securities for infrastructure projects in accordance with the prescribed rate under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to guarantee that the contractor shall perform his responsibilities in case of any structural defects/ failures within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: MDC, MPDO, BAC, Municipal Engineer and GSO provided explanations for noted deficiencies; some supporting documents submitted; some projects were inspected and actions for repairs/liquidated damages recommended.
observations.reason: Deferred implementation due to lack of prioritization and funding; documentation deficiencies persist; warranty securities practices not fully compliant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) MDRRMC properly plan for the PPAs out of LDRRMF and observe strict compliance with the time frame set by regulations in the preparation and operationalization of the LDRRMFIP to facilitate effective utilization of the LDRRMF in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002; (b) Municipal Mayor and MDRRMC strictly comply with the provisions of Section 22 (c) of RA 7160 and Quisumbing vs. Garcia on the utilization of lump sum appropriations by: [i] submitting the LDRRMFIP to the SB and requesting them to issue a resolution ratifying the expenditures amounting to ₱224,480.00; and [ii] ensure that prior authorization from the SB is secured for all the subsequent contracts under a lump sum appropriation, otherwise, the transactions will be deemed illegal pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003; (c) MDRRMC evaluate the PPAs under the unexpended GF-LDRRMF capital outlay and TF-DRRM, and reprogram the fund as deemed necessary to improve disaster resiliency of the Municipality, and include the same in the LDRRMFIP to establish its propriety in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002. Moreover, the MDRRMC, in the conduct of planning, [i] properly classify the PPAs according to its nature of disbursement; [ii] provide specific, measurable, achievable, realistic, and time-bound performance indicators to facilitate proper assessment of the DRRM capacity of the Municipality; and [iii] provide specific time-frame for the implementation of PPAs to facilitate strategic execution and monitoring of disaster-responsive measures programmed under the LDRRMFIP; (d) Municipal Accountant revert the unexpended 70% LDRRMF-CO amounting to ₱3.59M and the misclassified CO under the unspent 70% LDRRMF-MOOE totaling ₱600,000.00 to the GF in accordance with Section 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, and thereafter, provide proper disclosure of the DRRMF utilization and balances in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with Section 5.1.16 of the same circular for fair presentation; (e) MDRRMC issue a resolution to reclassify the CO expenditure items amounting to ₱600,000.00 erroneously classified under MOOE; (f) MDRRMO, Municipal Engineer and BAC fast track the implementation of the PPAs under LDRRMF by monitoring the period to which the same will be implemented and if applicable, pursuing its implementation in the year it was programmed and appropriated to improve the capacity of the Municipality to cope with potential hazards and disasters; and (g) MDRRMO provide a valid distribution list to account the exact amount of the pre-positioned goods and MDRRMC Resolution/s declaring necessity of the release of the stock piled goods, otherwise, the distribution would be deemed irregular under COA Circular No. 2012-003, maintain an organized inventory of DRRM equipment, supplies, and materials for proper safekeeping and to effectively monitor functionality and availability of the same should the need arises, in accordance with COA Circular No 2014-002, and seek the assistance of MGSO to provide proper identification to the properties by placing property numbers on DRRM equipment included in the inventory.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO submitted the LDRRMFIP albeit late; some PPAs were implemented; purchase of hydraulic excavator and other equipment were processed; MDRRMO agreed to prepare distribution lists and organize inventory; Municipal Accountant agreed to revert misclassified CO and provide disclosure in the Notes to FS.
observations.reason: Late preparation and submission of LDRRMFIP; non-inclusion of unexpended balances; erroneous transfers and misclassification of CO; many PPAs remained unimplemented; inventory and documentation issues persist.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and the Management agreed that the: • BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat submit explanation on the noted observations in the procurement process; • BAC Secretariat and the former OIC-Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents to avoid suspension in audit; and • Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that before payments, claims supported with necessary documents.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO submitted justification; some supporting documents (canvass, PR) were provided; BAC Secretariat to submit remaining documents; the equipment was delivered and accepted though documentation timing issues noted.
observations.reason: Non-conduct of pre-bid conference and incomplete procurement posting/documentation; performance security not posted; payment processed before all documents were in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the Municipality by educating their respective constituents through continuous IEC on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and the importance of their participation in the implementation of various SWM programs; (b) require the MENRO-Designate to (i) implement the minimum requirements in operating the existing dumpsite; (ii) coordinate with the concerned officials and employees for the provision for security fencing to prevent illegal entries for unauthorized disposal in the dumping site, (iii) exert effort to properly coordinate and prepare written request for the training of the operator/s for the technical operation of the composting facility with the source agency and secure copy of the signed MOA, and (iv) see to it that the shredder was not exposed to further deterioration since the housing for the equipment was not yet constructed; and (c) require the officials and employees concerned to prioritize and expedite the construction of RCA in order to comply with the requirements of DENR-EMB MIMAROPA for the approval of the SCRP and its subsequent implementation.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO-Designate coordinated for adoption of Ten-Year SWM Plan; all thirteen barangays established MRFs; MENRO plans to pursue RCA construction and training for composting facility; MENRO to secure MOA for use/operation of equipment.
observations.reason: Despite approval of SWM Plan and establishment of MRFs, RCA not yet constructed; composting facility inoperative due to lack of training and housing; monitoring of dumpsite inadequate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the: a. Local School Board (a) evaluate the PPAs in the SEF Budget by ensuring the compliance with the guidelines relative the utilization of SEF to priority items such as Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160, and (b) allocate funds for ECCD programs to conform with Section 7(b) of RA No. 10410 and DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017; and b. Officials and employees concerned provide justification for their failure to utilize the fund intended for the procurement of goods and construction of PPE, and ensure that the implementation of the PPAs will be properly monitored to ensure the timely delivery of needed services to the students and other beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board to review PPAs and ensure compliance; some PPAs remained unimplemented due to procurement delays and lack of counterpart funding; Management agreed to prioritize ECCD and SEF compliance.
observations.reason: Non-allocation for ECCD and inclusion of non-priority items; unutilized SEF appropriations due to procurement delays and insufficiency of counterpart funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. concerned officials and employees ensure that GFPS observe the scheduled GAD Planning and Budgeting as provided by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-001 dated January 12, 2016 to facilitate timely submission of GPB to the Provincial DILG for subsequent review and endorsement; b. Heads of implementing offices ensure that GAD programs, projects and activities are implemented as planned to attain the GAD objectives; and c. GFPS and GAD-TWG [i] gradually increase the gender-responsiveness of LGU programs and projects through attribution using the HGDG tool; [ii] attend capacity development trainings and workshops on GAD being administered by the PCW or DILG; thereafter, [iii] assist in the formulation of GAD Code; and [iv] strictly comply with the provisions of Section 36 of RA No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 dated July 18, 2013 to influence PPAs of the LGU toward gender-responsiveness.
observations.managementAction: GFPS was reorganized by EO No. 08 s. 2018; GPB was prepared and an approved GAD budget was reflected; however, GPB was submitted late for DILG review; some GAD PPAs were implemented and GAD database maintained.
observations.reason: GPB submitted late and not endorsed by DILG; GAD Code not yet formulated; only partial implementation of GPB PPAs and weak gender-analysis attribution.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the: (a) SB revisit the provisions embodied in the 1994 Revenue Code relative to Market fee imposition and policies and procedures on Market operations and make pertinent revisions/amendments thereto, if warranted; (b) Management ensure that the use and occupation on the Market stall units are covered with Contract of Lease and MOA for the collection of the stallholders’ counterpart for the facility improvements made by the Municipality to ensure that the terms and conditions agreed upon by both parties will be complied and their interests will be safeguarded; and devise plans and strategies to facilitate effective management of Market operations which may include: (i) creation of a Market Development Plan for the proper organization and classification of market sections; (ii) imposition of additional stall fees to lessees who continuously occupy excess spaces; (iii) issuance of demand letters to stallholders to encourage the timely payment of rental dues and avoidance of penalties; and (iv) issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Eviction to lessees who habitually and/or continuously violate the regulations set forth by the Revenue Code; (c) Designated Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer coordinate to enforce immediate & full collection of rental fees and corresponding surcharges from the delinquent stallholders; (d) Municipal Mayor make formal demand to Ms. Rosita Ramos and Ms. Nenita Puri to restitute the demolished partition walls, otherwise, file appropriate charges against them to indemnify damages sustained by the public market from the act of the stallholders; (e) Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit Monthly Report of Collection to the Accounting Office for proper performance monitoring and recording of receipts; and (f) Municipal Accountant prepare separate financial statements to account and monitor the operations of the Public Market and thereafter establish the appropriate receivable account/s to recognize the amount of accumulated uncollected stall rentals and unpaid counterpart dues for the installation of roll-up doors and disclose the nature of the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: Management noted recommendations and plans to create TWG to amend Revenue Code; intensify collections; issue Contracts of Lease to registered occupants; designate a Market Administrator; and take action regarding demolished partition walls.
observations.reason: Some improvements in collections noted but contracts of lease not fully executed and Revenue Code not yet amended; enforcement actions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer (a) submit justification/explanation for the failure to remit the taxes withheld under SEF and TF, and (b) continue withholding and remitting the monthly contributions and other amounts due within the prescribed period and analyze the balances for each account and make the necessary remittance or adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant mentioned they failed to remit taxes under SEF and TF because they are in the process of reconciliation with the BIR relative to over-remittance in CY 2017; they promised to comply with recommendations and remit contributions timely.
observations.reason: Late remittances occurred; reconciliation with BIR pending; remedial actions committed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MGSO-Designate abide by the rules set forth under Item 2.2.7 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to preserve public interest and remove markings of incumbent officials on government properties.
observations.managementAction: Markings were removed from some vehicles; MGSO to continue removal of markings.
observations.reason: Some markings were removed but not all; GSO action pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned AAR consider the option of adopting the automation of payroll system for the payment of wages of JO personnel. We also recommended the agency to exert control over cash handling and ensure that cash advances do not exceed MCA authorized under approved fidelity bond or apply for increased fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: The GSO installed biometric/machine attendance and advised monitoring of payroll; Municipality implemented measures to limit cash handling and encouraged banking of payroll.
observations.reason: Biometric attendance and payroll measures instituted; ATM/banking arrangements facilitated to reduce cash handling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that HRMO, in the meantime, use the biometric machine purchased and cautiously monitor logbook entries to ensure proper and accurate recording of attendance actually rendered by employees and job orders; require employees and officials as well as job orders to submit the DTRs based on bundy card/biometric print out signed by the immediate supervisor and attach to payroll; and OIC-Municipal Accountant scrutinize supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The biometric machine for JO employees was installed and being used in August 2018; reinstallation for permanent employees occurred in January 2019; HRMO to enforce usage and monitoring.
observations.reason: Biometric machine installed and used; DTRs and procedures instituted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and concerned municipal officials and employees include in the Program of Works (POWs) of the projects the specific pakyaw work required with the corresponding cost; submit proof/evidence that pakyaw labor was drawn from the vicinity of the project and present certificates of work completed; and comply with documentation requirements and submit lacking documents within five days to support propriety and validity of disbursements, and Municipal Accountant and Treasurer refrain from paying claims with incomplete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Some documentary requirements were later submitted; community meetings and registration of pakyaw groups to be documented for future projects.
observations.reason: Submission of necessary documentary requirements completed and processes instituted for future compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and BAC members and TWGs submit all the lacking documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made totaling ₱2,813,885.00 and ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and RA 9184 and its IRR; and Municipal Accountant and Treasurer refrain from paying claims with incomplete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The lacking supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team for verification.
observations.reason: Submission of missing documents completed; audit verification satisfied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and BAC of the members/TWGs submit all the lacking documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made totaling ₱503,850.00 to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit, and further ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and RA 9184 and its IRR; and Municipal Accountant and Treasurer refrain from paying claims with incomplete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Partial submission of the required documents was made; BAC and TWGs to submit remaining documents.
observations.reason: Some documentary requirements still pending (e.g., bid evaluation report, post-qualification report, performance security).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and MPDC monitor the implementation of the project and provide proof of delivery and whereabouts of materials; the Municipal Accountant and BAC/TWGs submit lacking documents within five days to support propriety and validity of disbursement; and refrain from paying claims with incomplete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Some documents were submitted and the Municipal Engineer provided statements of work accomplished; investigation on whereabouts of delivered materials to be pursued.
observations.reason: Incomplete submission of required documentation and unresolved issues on materials and project completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal SWDO, Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials submit the following documents to facilitate audit as to legality and propriety of the disbursements: justification of excess payments, Certificate of Appearance from Host LGUs, Terminal Report of Travel duly received by the DILG Regional Office, Photo Documentation of actual project implementation, approval from Host LGUs and DILG, itineraries of travel and reports of travel of participants; otherwise, transaction would be considered irregular and refund of the full amount shall be made.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted various documents and the Municipal Officials refunded excess per diem where applicable; Notice of Suspension and Disallowance issued to some claims.
observations.reason: Some required documents submitted and partial refunds made; other documentation still lacking and some disbursements subject to ND/NS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the participants refund the total amount of ₱30,000.00 for the excess in total per diem of ₱800 as per EO 298 (Travel Law) for claiming ₱2,000 each for food and lodging for the two-day activity; and Municipal Officials to observe austerity measures.
observations.managementAction: Request for refund of excess per diem made by concerned participants; some refunds processed.
observations.reason: Some refunds made; other participants yet to refund and some documentation still outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality discontinue the practice of providing appropriation and disbursing public funds for religious or private purposes and strictly adhere to applicable laws, rules and regulations in the disbursement of government funds; Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to scrutinize claims and ensure support before payment.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted explanations and some supporting documents; participants and organizers to observe austerity measures and provide additional justifications.
observations.reason: Some justification provided; full documentary compliance not achieved and issue reiterated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRM Council properly plan, prioritize and evaluate PPAs to be funded out of LDRRMF to attain the objective and purpose of the Fund; reassess the necessity of pursuing unimplemented projects and reprogram the same; ensure MDRRMC resolutions for amendments; and fast track implementation.
observations.managementAction: Some reallocations and procurement steps initiated; MDRRMO to prepare revised LDRRMFIP and MDRRMC to review PPAs.
observations.reason: PPAs not prioritized and many remained unimplemented; MDRRMC to reassess and reprogram as needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman and members explain the noted observations on the procurement; the Management seek justification from the DSDO relative to delay in delivery of outputs; Municipal Accountant compute liquidated damages for delay and make formal demand; ensure compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Memorandum and RIRR of RA 9184; Municipal Budget Officer provide justification for non-reversion of CY2015 appropriation and Municipal Accountant indicate source of fund in future CAF issuances.
observations.managementAction: Partial submission of required documents; some explanations provided; legal/admin follow-up pending.
observations.reason: Deficiencies in procurement documents and failure to revert appropriations; actions pending and documentation incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor observe austerity measures, former Municipal Mayor submit liquidation of cash assistance or refund, OIC Accountant and concerned officials submit lacking documents to facilitate audit and Municipal Accountant and Treasurer scrutinize claims and ensure necessary supporting documents prior to payment.
observations.managementAction: Some lacking documents were submitted; former Municipal Mayor refunded part of the assistance and other reconciliations pursued.
observations.reason: Some refunds made and documents submitted; outstanding issues remain and further liquidation required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require immediate restitution of remaining funds relative to the initial Demand Letter issued by the Audit Team and based on the report of the Municipal Accountant, if any, without prejudice to the filing of appropriate charges against the former Municipal Treasurer, and further investigate discrepancies and prepare reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer made partial restitution; claim reconciliations prepared and further collection/restitution to be pursued; assessment and charges considered.
observations.reason: Partial restitution of funds made; further reconciliation and possible actions pending against former Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant withhold taxes on the honoraria paid and that BAC, TWG and MSWDO submit necessary supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001; BAC and TWG to comply with DBM budget circulars and COA guidelines in claiming honoraria; Municipal Accountant to charge to appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction: Withholding of taxes instituted for subsequent payments; BAC/TWG and MSWDO instructed to submit supporting documents and comply with guidelines; some required documents submitted.
observations.reason: Withholding of taxes implemented and documentation process strengthened.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the accounts affected and determine necessary adjusting entries to reconcile Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts to fairly present the same in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started analysis and reconciliation of intra-agency accounts and adjusted some balances, decreasing unreconciled amounts to ₱1,128,958.88 Due from Other Funds vs ₱1,619,702.97 Due to Other Funds.
observations.reason: Analysis started; unreconciled balances decreased but remain outstanding and further reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: the Municipal Mayor create and establish an Inventory Committee, headed by the OIC-GSO with representatives from the Accounting, Engineering and Assessor’s Office, which will prepare a plan for the conduct of a complete physical inventory including the (a) time frame to complete inventory count and the (b) preparation of the report taking into consideration the present condition and location of the properties. ARE must be made available for fast tracing of the properties and should be updated to establish proper accountability. The OIC-GSO should also obtain from the Accounting Office the listing of properties per category of PPE as reference in the inventory taking; Thereafter, The OIC-Accountant and OIC-GSO coordinate and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the proper reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties; Moreover, The OIC-Accountant to maintain Property Ledgers that will provide a complete breakdown of the amounts reflected in the Financial Statements, effect proper adjustments in the books for items that necessitate corrections as identified in the verification and reconciliation process, create separate Subsidiary Ledgers for the unreconciled account balances and observe proper disclosure of the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation. Municipal Mayor establish an Inventory Committee as part of the Municipality’s Local Roads Asset Management System, members of which includes, but are not limited to: The OIC-GSO for property custodianship and reporting of the Local Road Network System; The Municipal Engineer for technical assistance in the cost valuation and segregation of the Road Network and its components; OIC-Accountant for proper accounting of the Local Roads in line with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015. Municipal Engineer prepare a schedule of the Local Roads indicating therein the cost of each Road Network Component such as (a) road lot, (b) road pavement, (c) drainage and slope protection structures, (d) and other miscellaneous structures to be submitted to the GSO and Accounting Office. Moreover, subsequent road projects of the Municipality should clearly indicate the separate cost of each road component for easy reference. The Engineering Office is also vested to determine the life of the Local Roads taking into consideration the (1) design, (2) type of users, and (3) volume of users of the roads. Such detail should also be included in the schedule of Local Roads to serve basis for the depreciation of the Road Components to be applied by the Accounting Office; OIC-GSO prepare and maintain R P C L R N and Road Map to account for the Local Roads. These records should be reconciled with the Registry of Public Infrastructures to be prepared by the by the OIC-Accountant; and OIC-Accountant prepare and maintain RPI for all Public Infrastructure Assets, trace back records of the completed Infrastructure Assets starting from the current year backwards. Additionally, prepare separate Subsidiary Ledgers for the determined cost of the Road Network Components, and thereafter provide for the depreciation of each Road Component based from the records to be prepared by the Engineering Office to establish proper valuation of the Road Network.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee. Partial inventory count was executed by the Property and Accounting Offices during January 2018.
observations.reason: Inability to perform a complete inventory due to lack of manpower and insufficiency or unavailability of records as to details of procurement, whereabouts and conditions of the properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: The Municipal Assessor, OIC-GSO and OIC-Treasurer coordinate obtain documents necessary for titling the parcels of land accounted in the name of the Municipality; and The OIC-Budget Officer annually appropriate fund on a per parcel basis to ensure that the Municipality’s budget can accommodate the survey and titling of each land in the name of the Municipality to preserve legal rights over the real properties and to avoid exposure to third party claims.
observations.managementAction: Management has not taken any action yet.
observations.reason: Inability to secure necessary documents to effect transfer of legal ownership.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Human Resources and Management Officer (HRMO) ensure that the functions and responsibilities of the JO personnel are specified in their Job Order contract for proper evaluation of their work and accomplishments and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation/audit within the prescribed period. Moreover, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that: the Municipal Mayor through the help of HRMO create a committee that will formulate the guidelines on the hiring of JO personnel for the proper recruitment and selection to meet the requirements of the position; require the Department Head where those JOs are assigned to submit written justification for the necessity of hiring them and maximize the services of those personnel holding permanent positions to do regular, administrative and clerical functions; and a policy guideline setting the salary rates for the JO personnel must be established to properly compensate the services they have provided.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken yet by the management.
observations.reason: Non-formulation of a well-defined policy guidelines on recruitment and selection of personnel through Job Order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal government consider the option of adopting the automation of payroll system for the payment of wages of JO personnel. We also recommend the review of volume and amount of transactions drawn thru cash advances by Disbursing Officer and see to it that the same shall not exceed the MCA authorized under her approved fidelity bond or else apply for an increased fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO was still in the process of coordinating with the service provider (LBP Pinamalayan Branch).
observations.reason: Insufficiency/ unavailability of necessary documents required by LBP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management prepare Market Development Plan which will serve as guide for the policies and procedures in the operations of the Market since there was still no amendments in the existing Revenue Code and reiterated our recommendations that the: Municipal Mayor and other officials and employees concerned devise a system or strategy for the effective management of market operations particularly in the proper classification and numbering of market stalls, encouraging the lessees/stallholders to occupy only their leased spaces, and pay their rental fees in a timely manner as provided in the Revenue Code of the Municipality; Sangguniang Bayan revisit the provisions embodied in the 1994 Revenue Code relative to the market fees and policies and procedures for market operations and make revisions or amendments, when warranted; Lease of the units of the market stalls in the public market should be covered with Contract of Lease, and MOA for the collection of the stall holders’ counterpart for the improvement of rented stalls to ensure that the terms and conditions agreed upon by both parties will be complied and their interests will be safeguarded; Management strictly enforce the collection of fees and corresponding surcharges from the delinquent stallholders and report to the Municipal Accountant the accumulated uncollected amounts for the proper taking up of the uncollected market stall fees that are deemed realizable to reflect the accurate amount of receivable in the financial statements; and Designated Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer coordinate to enforce immediate & full collection of rental fees and corresponding surcharges from the delinquent stallholders.
observations.managementAction: There was still no amendment in the Revenue Code relative to the provisions for market operations and no Market Development Plan being formulated because the concerned officials and employees was still worrying for the insufficiency of the funds that will be incurred for the development of market.
observations.reason: Continuous failure of the Sangguniang Bayan to prioritize the amendment of the outdated provisions of the Revenue Code; and Continuous failure of the Executive Administration to execute any binding contract of lease with the stallholders and to devise a defined market development plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials and employees explain why the MDC Planning Workshop and Approval of AIP 2017 and Adoption of MDIP 2017-2019 was conducted at City State Hotel on August 24 to 26, 2016 and not within the Municipality/Province. We likewise recommend the 75 participants to refund the total amount of ₱30,000.00 for the excess in the total per diem of ₱800 as per EO 298 (Travel Law) for claiming ₱2,000.00 each for food and lodging for the two-day activity. Furthermore, the Municipal Officials strictly observe the austerity measures set forth by DILG Administrative Order No. 103 series of 2004 dated August 31, 2004 to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken. None of the requested documents were submitted to substantiate validity of the disbursement made.
observations.reason: Failure to submit and comply the requested documentation necessary to validate the disbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board through MENRO-Designate (i) submit the required documents for the approval of plan; (ii) monitor and evaluate the implementation of the SWM programs and policies; (iii) monitor the strict implementation of segregation of wastes at source; and (iv) stop the practice of open dumping in the Municipality to prevent the consequences of the violation of Section 37 of RA 9003 with the corresponding fines and penalties indicated in Section 49 of the same Act. Likewise, develop strategies on how to improve the existing disposal facility to reduce adverse impacts on health and environment and prevent the issuance of Notice to Sue by the DENR. Also, an ECC must be secured from the DENR relative to the operation of the dumping site. We also recommended that the requirements of RA 9003 relative to the installation of Material Recovery Facilities must be observed for the efficient sorting and storing of wastes.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is still practicing open dumping of solid wastes and the SWM Plan was still conditionally approved for failure to include other requirements by the NSWMC.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower that will help in the inclusion of the requirements in the SWM Plan for the same to be approved by the NSWMC and additional manpower that will monitor the proper segregation of waste. The assigned backhoe in the dumpsite usually malfunctions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal government officials and employees optimally utilized the fund by improving the current situation of the disposal site and establishing the MRF and as required by Sections 32 and 33 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken.
observations.reason: Non-prioritization on by the current Administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: the BAC Chairman and the members of the BAC explain the noted observations in the procurement process relative to the procurement of services for the preparation of technical survey description and plans for the proposed subdivision; the Management require the Dayanghirang Surveying and Development Office to submit the necessary supporting documents as part of the performance of the scope of work as indicated in the contract agreement in support to the claim for partial payment and the BAC Secretariat submit the lacking bid documents to support the propriety of the disbursement and to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit; the Management seek justification from the DSDO relative to the delay in the delivery of the expected output which was the Consolidation/Subdivision Plan approved by the DENR-LMS and LRA which will eventually be used in the implementation of housing project for the Municipal officials and employees; the Municipal Accountant compute for the liquidated damages incurred for the delay in the delivery of services and make a formal demand against the supplier for the payment of the amount computed as required in Section 68 of Revised IRR of RA 9184; and the Management strictly comply with the provisions of the Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445, COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 and Revised IRR of RA 9184 relative to the procurement of consulting services for the preparation of technical survey description and plans. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer provide justification relative to the non-reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the CY 2015 appropriation for Survey and Titling Expenses under MOOE as required in Section 322 of RA 7160; and the Municipal Accountant indicate the source of fund in the future issuances of Certificate of Availability of Funds for proper audit of the same.
observations.managementAction: None of the requested documents were submitted to substantiate validity of the disbursement made.
observations.reason: Lack of knowledge of the BAC members and TWG in the procurement of consultancy services.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to analyze, confirm and ascertain the validity of the long outstanding liabilities in the Trust Fund book and if found with valid claims notify the claimants, for other payables account, if found without valid documentation and/or which have been outstanding for more than two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed, proper adjustment in the books of accounts can be made and return the amount to the source agencies or to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing verification of the records.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and insufficiency/ unavailability of documents.

observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our recommendation that the:

         ➢ Municipal Mayor create and establish an Inventory and  Appraisal
           Committee, headed by  the  GSO  with  representatives  from  the
           Accounting,  Engineering  and  Assessor’s  Office,  which   will
           prepare a plan for the conduct of a complete physical  inventory
           including the (i) time frame to complete inventory count and the
           (ii) preparation of the report, taking  into  consideration  the
           present condition and location of the properties.  ARE  must  be
           made available for fast tracing of the properties and should  be
           updated to establish proper accountability. The  OIC-GSO  should
           also obtain from the Accounting Office the listing of properties
           per category of  PPE  as  reference  in  the  inventory  taking;
           Thereafter,


         ➢  Municipal  Accountant  and  OIC-GSO  coordinate   and   prepare
           supporting schedules to facilitate the proper reconciliation  of
           the property accounts and  establishment  of  actual  existence,
           ownership and condition of the properties; and the


         ➢  Municipal  Accountant  maintain  Property  Ledgers  that   will
           provide a complete breakdown of the  amounts  reflected  in  the
           Financial Statements, effect proper adjustments in the books for
           items  that  necessitate  corrections  as  identified   in   the
           verification  and  reconciliation   process,   create   separate
           Subsidiary Ledgers for the  unreconciled  account  balances  and
           observe proper disclosure of the same in the Notes to  Financial
           Statements for fair presentation.

        We reiterated our recommendations that the:


         ➢ Municipal Mayor establish an Inventory Committee as part of  the
           Municipality’s Local Roads Asset Management System,  members  of
           which includes, but are not limited to:


            • The GSO for property custodianship and reporting of the Local
              Road Network System;
            • The Municipal Engineer for technical assistance in  the  cost
              valuation  and  segregation  of  the  Road  Network  and  its
              components;
            • Municipal Accountant for proper accounting of the Local Roads
              in line with the provisions  of  COA  Circular  No.  2015-008
              dated November 23, 2015.


         ➢ Municipal  Engineer  prepare  a  schedule  of  the  Local  Roads
           indicating therein the cost of each Road Network Component  such
           as (a) road lot, (b)  road  pavement,  (c)  drainage  and  slope
           protection structures, (d) and other miscellaneous structures to
           be  submitted  to  the  GSO  and  Accounting  Office.  Moreover,
           subsequent road projects  of  the  Municipality  should  clearly
           indicate the separate cost  of  each  road  component  for  easy
           reference. The Engineering Office is also  vested  to  determine
           the life of the Local Roads taking into  consideration  the  (1)
           design, (2) type of users, and (3) volume of users of the roads.
           Such detail should also be included in  the  schedule  of  Local
           Roads to serve basis for the depreciation of the Road Components
           to be applied by the Accounting Office;


         ➢ OIC-GSO prepare and maintain RPCLRN and Road Map to account  for
           the Local Roads. These records should  be  reconciled  with  the
           Registry of Public Infrastructures to be prepared by the by  the
           Municipal Accountant; and


         ➢ Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain  RPI  for  all  Public
           Infrastructure Assets,  trace  back  records  of  the  completed
           Infrastructure Assets starting from the current year  backwards.
           Additionally,  prepare  separate  Subsidiary  Ledgers  for   the
           determined cost of the Road Network Components,  and  thereafter
           provide for the depreciation of each Road Component  based  from
           the  records  to  be  prepared  by  the  Engineering  Office  to
           establish proper valuation of the Road Network.

We  recommended  that  the  concerned  Management   officials   and
   employees:


        a.  Submit  satisfactory  explanation  relative   the   above-noted
           observations in the grant of financial assistance;


        b. Regulate the grant of financial assistance to the most essential
           only and ensure that funds disbursed are properly  utilized  for
           public welfare and  refrain  from  giving  financial  assistance
           which does not conform with the exigencies of the service;

        c.  Ensure  that  future  claims   be   supported   with   complete
           documentation and exercise  the  prudence  as  required  by  the
           service; and

        d. Accomplish the General Intake Sheet properly by filling  up  the
           appropriate sections of the GIS.

        We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor,  in  giving  financial
   assistance (a) strictly adhere to the guidelines of the Municipality  and
   (b) use the assessment report of the MSWDO in determining the  amount  to
   be given to the beneficiaries.

            We recommended that the:


         ➢ Concerned Municipal Officials and  Employees  conform  with  the
           prescribes rules and regulations in the rates of allowances  for
           official local travels as indicated in EO No. 298; and


         ➢ Municipal Accountant and  OIC-Treasurer  enforce  immediate  and
           full  refund  of  the  excess  travelling   allowance   totaling
           ₱69,619.00. In case of non-compliance by the listed  AOs,  cause
           withholding of their  salary  until  full  settlement  of  their
           accountability has been rendered.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the
   concerned Municipal government officials and employees optimally utilized
   the fund by improving the current situation  of  the  disposal  site  and
   establishing the MRF and as required by Sections 32 and 33 of RA 9003.

      We reiterated our recommendation that the:

      ➢ Sangguniang Bayan revisit  the  provisions  embodied  in  the  1994
        Revenue Code  relative  to  (i)  Market  fee  imposition  and  (ii)
        policies and procedures on Market  operations  and  make  pertinent
        revisions/amendments thereto, if warranted;


      ➢ Tenancy on the Market stall  units  be  covered  with  Contract  of
        Lease and MOA for the collection of the  stallholders’  counterpart
        for the facility improvements made by the  Municipality  to  ensure
        that the terms and conditions agreed upon by both parties  will  be
        complied and their interests will be safeguarded;

      ➢ Local Administration devise  plans  and  strategies  to  facilitate
        effective management of Market operations  which  may  include  (i)
        creation of a Market Development Plan for the  proper  organization
        and  classification  of  market  sections;   (ii)   imposition   of
        additional stall fees to lessees  who  continuously  occupy  excess
        spaces;  (iii)  issuance  of  demand  letters  to  stallholders  to
        encourage the timely  payment  of  rental  dues  and  avoidance  of
        penalties; (iv) issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Eviction to
        lessees who habitually and/or continuously violate the  regulations
        set forth by the Revenue Code; and


      ➢ Designated Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer  coordinate
        to  enforce  immediate  &  full  collection  of  rental  fees   and
        corresponding surcharges from the delinquent stallholders;

   We also recommended that  the  (a)  Market  Administrator  and  Municipal
   Treasurer  prepare  and  submit  Monthly  Report  of  Collection  to  the
   Accounting Office for the proper performance monitoring and recording  of
   receipts;  and  (b)  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  separate   Financial
   Statements to account for and monitor the operations of the Public Market
   and  thereafter  establish  the  appropriate  receivable   account/s   to
   recognize the amount of accumulated uncollected stall rentals and  unpaid
   counterpart dues for the installation of roll-up doors and  disclose  the
   nature of the  same  in  the  Notes  to  Financial  Statements  for  fair
   presentation.

We reiterated our recommendation that and the Management  agreed  that
      the MDRRMO prepare and submit the monthly Report on  the  Sources  and
      Utilization of LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular  No.  2012-002  to
      the Office of the Auditor for the timely review and evaluation of  the
      same to attain the transparency of and accountability in  the  use  of
      LDRRMF.

      We recommended and the Management agreed that the MDRRMO (a)  maintain
      an organized inventory of DRRM equipment, supplies and  materials  for
      the efficient management of the same; and (b) seek the  assistance  of
      GSO in the proper identification by placing property numbers for those
      equipment included in the inventory.

        We  recommended  that  the  MDRRMO   properly   account   for   the
   distribution of the  stockpiled  goods  and  make  the  necessary  refund
   equivalent to the cost of relief goods for those goods which are released
   not  in  accordance  with  the  purpose  stated  in  the  above-mentioned
   guidelines. Moreover, the MDRRMO observe the  guidelines  in  the  future
   distribution of the goods intended for prepositioning.

            We recommended that the:

         • BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat submit satisfactory explanation  on
           the noted observations in the procurement  process  relative  to
           the acquisition of the rescue vehicle;


         • BAC Secretariat and  Municipal  Accountant  submit  the  lacking
           supporting documents for audit purposes; and


         •  members  of  the  Inspection   Committee   and   GSO   properly
           accomplished the IAR by indicating the dates for  the  efficient
           and effective evaluation of the documents and  transactions  and
           issue the corresponding ARE to MDRRMO  for  the  rescue  vehicle
           which is under his custody since acquisition.

We recommended that the:

         • BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat submit satisfactory explanation  on
           the noted observations in the procurement  process  relative  to
           the acquisition of the rescue vehicle;


         • BAC Secretariat and  Municipal  Accountant  submit  the  lacking
           supporting documents for audit purposes; and


         •  members  of  the  Inspection   Committee   and   GSO   properly
           accomplished the IAR by indicating the dates for  the  efficient
           and effective evaluation of the documents and  transactions  and
           issue the corresponding ARE to MDRRMO  for  the  rescue  vehicle
           which is under his custody since acquisition.

We recommended that the:

         • BAC Secretariat and  Municipal  Accountant  submit  the  lacking
           supporting documents within five days to support  the  propriety
           and validity of the disbursement made;


         • BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat submit satisfactory explanation  on
           the  noted  observations  in  the  procurement  process  of  the
           subjected transactions and accomplished the  documents  properly
           to avoid confusion, especially on the date of delivery; and

         • Municipal Mayor make a formal demand against  Three  Star  Frame
           Trading for  the  amount  of  liquidated  damages  amounting  to
           ₱8,923.52.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant agreed to:


         • Exert  effort  to  determine  the  validity  of  the  amount  of
           ₱302,003.87 of General Fund- Savings account;


         • secure copies of  bank  debit  and  credit  memos  so  that  the
           necessary adjustments in the General, SEF, and Trust Fund  books

observations.recommendation:
