observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
the Municipal Mayor create and establish an Inventory Committee, headed by the OIC-GSO with representatives from the Accounting, Engineering and Assessor’s Office, which will prepare a plan for the conduct of a complete physical inventory including the (a) time frame to complete inventory count and the (b) preparation of the report taking into consideration the present condition and location of the properties. ARE must be made available for fast tracing of the properties and should be updated as found necessary to establish proper accountability. The OIC-GSO should also obtain from the Accounting Office a listing of properties per category of PPE as reference in the inventory taking; Thereafter,
The OIC-Accountant and OIC-GSO coordinate and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the proper reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties; Moreover,
The OIC-Accountant to maintain Property Ledgers that will provide a complete breakdown of the amounts reflected in the Financial Statements, effect proper adjustments in the books for items that necessitate corrections as identified in the verification and reconciliation process, create separate Subsidiary Ledgers for the unreconciled account balances and observe proper disclosure of the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Mayor establish an Inventory Committee as part of the Municipality’s Local Roads Asset Management System, members of which includes, but are not limited to:
The OIC-GSO for property custodianship and reporting of the Local Road Network System;
The Municipal Engineer for technical assistance in the cost valuation and segregation of the Road Network and its components;
OIC-Accountant for proper accounting of the Local Roads in line with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
Municipal Engineer prepare a schedule of the Local Roads indicating therein the cost of each Road Network Component such as (a) road lot, (b) road pavement, (c) drainage and slope protection structures, (d) and other miscellaneous structures to be submitted to the GSO and Accounting Office. Moreover, subsequent road projects of the Municipality should clearly indicate the separate cost of each road component for easy reference.
The Engineering Office is also vested to determine the life of the Local Roads taking into consideration the (1) design, (2) type of users, and (3) volume of users of the roads. Such detail should also be included in the schedule of Local Roads to serve basis for the depreciation of the Road Components to be applied by the Accounting Office.
GSO prepare and maintain RLRN and Road Map to account for the Local Roads. These records should be reconciled with the Registry of Public Infrastructures to be prepared by the by the OIC-Accountant.
OIC-Accountant prepare and maintain RPI for all Public Infrastructure Assets, trace back records of the completed Infrastructure Assets starting from the current year backwards. Additionally, to prepare separate Subsidiary Ledgers for the determined cost of the Road Network Components, and thereafter provide for the depreciation of each Road Component based from the records to be prepared by the Engineering Office to establish proper valuation of the Road Network.

We recommended that the:
The Municipal Assessor, OIC-GSO and OIC-Treasurer coordinate obtain documents necessary for titling the parcels of land accounted in the name of the Municipality; and the
The OIC-Budget Officer annually appropriate funds on a per parcel basis to ensure that the Municipality’s budget can accommodate the survey and titling of each land in the name of the Municipality to preserve legal rights over the real properties and to avoid exposure to third party claims.
We recommended that the OIC-GSO abide the with the rules set forth under Item 2.2.7 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to preserve public interest.

We recommended that the OIC-GSO mark the service vehicles with “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” pursuant to the requirements of the regulations to avoid possible use of government vehicles other than their official functions.

We recommended that the concerned SB Members provide explanation as to the noted observations relative to their attendance in the NEO-NB Meeting and 8th series of CCLEP of the PCL and the Municipal Mayor may file appropriate charges for the respective SB Members for falsifying the official documents and report the same to the Civil Service Commission for further legal action.
We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that all claims must be supported with complete and valid documentation, in case of the liquidation of CAs, scrutinize the Liquidation Reports and see to it that it was supported with valid documents as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 before the preparation of a particular Journal Entry Voucher taking up the liquidation was made.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant monitor the liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period as prescribed by law and that the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer disallow the additional cash advance by the Municipal Officials and Employees with pending unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that Human Resources and Management Officer (HRMO) ensure that the functions and responsibilities of the JO personnel are specified in their Job Order contract for proper evaluation of their work and accomplishments and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation/audit within the prescribed period. Moreover, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that :
the Municipal Mayor through the help of HRMO create a committee that will formulate the guidelines on the hiring of JO personnel for the proper recruitment and selection to meet the requirements of the position;
require the Department Head where those JOs are assigned to submit written justification for the necessity of hiring them and maximize the services of those personnel holding permanent positions to do regular, administrative and clerical functions; and
a policy guideline setting the salary rates for the JO personnel must be established to properly compensate the services they have provided.

We recommended that the concerned Municipal government consider the option of adopting the automation of payroll system for the payment of wages of JO personnel. We also recommend the review of volume and amount of transactions drawn thru cash advances by Disbursing Officer and see to it that the same shall not exceed the MCA authorized under her approved fidelity bond or else apply for an increased fidelity bond.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Engineer coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of the PPAs to maximize the utilization of 20% DF;
Municipal Development Council with the assistance of the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented projects, otherwise reprogram the same to a more needed developmental projects. The Council should also properly plan and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year; and
Municipal Development Council and Budget Officer appropriate sufficient fund for the complete implementation of the identified projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and BAC members and TWGs submit all the lacking documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made totaling ₱2,813,885.0, and further ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and RA 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant and BAC members/TWGs submit all the lacking documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made totaling ₱503,850.00 to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit, and further ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and RA 9184 and its IRR; and
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer refrain from paying claims with incomplete documentation.

We recommended that the municipal officials and employees explain why the MDC Planning Workshop and Approval of AIP 2017 and Adoption of MDIP 2017-2019 was conducted  at City State Hotel on August 24 to 26, 2016 and not within the Municipality/Province. We likewise recommend the 75 participants to refund the total amount of ₱30,000.00 for the excess in the total per diem of ₱800 as per EO 298 (Travel Law) for claiming ₱2,000.00 each for food and lodging for the two days activity.
Furthermore, the Municipal Officials strictly observe the austerity measures set forth by DILG Administrative Order No. 103 series of 2004 dated August 31, 2004 to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the:
Municipality discontinue the practice of providing appropriation and disbursing public funds for religious or private purposes and strictly adhere with the applicable laws, rules and regulations in the disbursement of government funds; and
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer scrutinize the payment of the claims and see to it that it was supported with necessary supporting documents before payment was made.

We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board through MENRO-Designate (i) submit the required documents for the approval of plan; (ii) monitor and evaluate the implementation of the SWM programs and policies; (iii) monitor the strict implementation of segregation of wastes at source; and (iv) stop the practice of open dumping in the Municipality to prevent the consequences of the violation of Section 37 of RA 9003 with the corresponding fines and penalties indicated in Section 49 of the same Act.
Likewise, develop strategies on how to improve the existing disposal facility to reduce adverse impacts on health and environment and prevent the issuance of Notice to Sue by the DENR. Also, an ECC must be secured from the DENR relative to the operation of the dumping site.
We also recommended that the requirements of RA 9003 relative to the installation of Material Recovery Facilities must be observed for the efficient sorting and storing of wastes.

We recommended that the concerned Municipal government officials and employees optimally utilized the fund by improving the current situation of the disposal site and establishing the MRF and as required by Sections 32 and 33 of RA 9003.

We recommended that the MDRRM Council properly plan, prioritize and evaluate the programs, projects, and activities to be funded out of the LDRRMF to attain the objective and purpose of the Fund by observing the rules and regulations relative thereto.
We also recommended that the Council reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented projects, otherwise reprogram the same to those PPAs which will help build the disaster resilience of communities and will lessen the impact of deadly and costly natural and man-made hazards/disasters with the goal of promoting sustainable socio-economic growth.
Likewise, the MDRRMC must see to it that in case there are changes in the previously approved MDRRMFIP, MDRRMC resolution must be prepared to support the amendments.
We recommend that the Management allocate the required 1% of their IRA and likewise abide to the rules and regulations with regards to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze and make pertinent adjustments to each affected “Accumulated Depreciation” accounts for the proper valuation of the property accounts, and thereafter disclose the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adopted the 5% salvage value of depreciable assets, recomputed the depreciation expense and effected the adjustments in the books.
observations.reason:
Insufficiency of documents/
information on the remaining properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the Municipal Accountant and the OIC- Treasurer investigate the discrepancies noted, prepare a reconciliation statement and report the same to the Municipal Mayor; and

the Municipal Mayor require immediate restitution of remaining fund relative to the initial Demand Letter issued by the Audit Team and based on the report of the Municipal Accountant, if any, without prejudice to the  filing of appropriate charges against the former Municipal Treasurer, Ms. Estrella E. del Mundo, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Management required the former MT to restitute the fund and paid another P1.2 million in June 2016
observations.reason: The balance of more than ₱400,000.00 based on the review of transactions made by the Accounting Office, is not yet restituted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the Municipal Accountant conduct thorough monitoring and review of the cash advances and their pertinent liquidations to avoid excess claims by the AOs; and

the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer issue demand letters for the refund of excess travelling allowances claimed by the AOs concerned.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assigned a personnel to monitor the cash advances.
observations.reason: The overpayment was not yet refunded but the former MT agreed to  deduct the overpayment from her terminal leave benefit.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assigned a personnel to monitor the cash advances.
observations.reason: Weak internal control of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period as prescribed by law and that the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer prohibit drawing of additional cash advance by the Municipal Officials and Employees if previously granted cash advance was not yet fully settled/liquidated.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assigned a personnel to monitor the cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that: 
All Officials and Employees granted with cash advances refrain from using the cash advances for purposes other than what is intended for; and
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer strictly monitor compliance by the Officials and Employees to the provisions of Section 4.1.7 of COA Circular No. 97-002 regarding the use of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assigned a personnel to monitor the cash advances. Cash advances that will be found used other than the purpose it was granted will not be considered liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the rules and regulations in the maintenance of special accounts in the General Fund through the use of complete subsidiary ledgers and prepare separate set of financial statements for each economic enterprise and 20% Development Fund to assess their financial performance and determine their financial position.
We also recommended that the concerned municipal officials conduct feasibility study on the operation of those enterprises and the results thereto should be forwarded to the Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan for the possible creation of such economic enterprise with separate budget and special account in the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is in the process of analyzing the transactions pertaining to economic enterprises for the preparation/ maintenance of separate SL.
observations.reason: Change of Management and the new LCE is still evaluating the capacity of personnel to be assigned in the operations of LEEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MDRRMO ensure that issuances of relief goods and construction materials and other relief operations are properly documented. Likewise, the GSO through the help of the MDRRMO conduct the required inventory of items and prepare corresponding reports to ascertain the balances of the relief goods and construction materials that remained on hand as of year-end, and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the report to reflect the correct amount of inventories. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF to present fairly the expense and inventory accounts in the Financial Statements.
 
We also recommended that the officials and employees concerned in the Accounting and Treasury department scrutinize the claims and see to it that the required supporting documents were attached before approving the claims against the government funds.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO exerted effort to monitor the issuances of the relief goods and prepare the photo documentation of the issuances.
observations.reason: Inventory of relief goods was not yet completed due to lack of manpower and there were issuances that the MSWDO was in-charge.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MDRRM Council with the assistance of the MDRRMO revisit the LDRRMFIP of the Municipality and see to it that it was prepared according to the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002. 
We also recommended that for subsequent preparation of Investment plan, the MDRRM Council ensure that the prepared plan includes the proposed PPAs out of current appropriations and the unexpended balance of fund transferred to Trust Fund and see to it that the  priority DRRM programs, projects and activities being implemented are included in the plan.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant  transferred the balance of unobligated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Fund book under Trust Liability-DRRM account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper accounting entries in recording the disbursements out of Trust Liability-DRRM and in the recognition of liability in the books of accounts.  Moreover, draw Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the transactions for the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already recorded the transaction to appropriate accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the  Municipal Accountant and Treasurer withhold taxes on the honoraria paid to BAC and TWG members and the Municipal Coordinating Team under KALAHI CIDSS Program in compliance with existing BIR Revenue Regulations;

the BAC and TWG strictly comply with the regulations provided in DBM Budget Circular Nos. 2004-5A and 2007-3 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in claiming honoraria of personnel involved in government procurement; and

the Municipal Accountant, BAC and TWG submit the necessary supporting documents required in Item 5.1.7 of  the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to prove the accuracy, propriety and validity of the amounts collected and ensure that the same claims in the future be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Honoraria given to BAC and TWG were subjected to withholding of taxes in accordance with BIR regulations.
observations.reason: Incomplete documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the  nine SB Officials submit the original Official Receipts stated in the original and photocopied Certificate of Appearance and the original copy of the Certificate of Appearance; and

the Municipal Accountant ensure that claims in the future be supported with complete and valid documentation
observations.managementAction: The SB members refunded the amount claimed.
observations.reason: Some of the SB members continuously liquidate CAs for attendance to PCL activities using ORs issued to different individuals.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the preparation of the Annual Budget, the Municipal Budget Officer correctly classify into Other General Services account the appropriation for the wages of the various JO personnel under the Office of the Municipal Engineer and KALAHI CIDSS program. Moreover, we also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the cost of the wages in its appropriate expense account to avoid misclassification of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office reclassified the cost of wages in its appropriate expense account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officials and employees concerned to prepare the required reports in the proper formats and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor in the period as prescribed by the different rules and regulations for audit and validation purposes.
observations.managementAction: The SAAOB, Monthly Monitoring Report of the awarded infrastructure projects, programs and activities (PPAs) and List of all On-going Government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, and Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms  were submitted.
observations.reason: Inventory Reports and some BRS were not yet submitted due to lack of complete property records and manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the accounts affected and determine the necessary adjusting entries to reconcile the  Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts to fairly present the same in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have already started analysis of the accounts affected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 
Municipal Development Council and Budget Officer appropriate enough fund for the complete implementation of the identified projects, refrain from appropriating funds on a staggered basis,  and ensure that projects under the 20% development fund conform with the provisions of the DILG-DBM JMC no. 2011-1; 
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) monitor, evaluate and when necessary, suggest to concerned municipal officials appropriate action  for the implementation of the identified development PPAs; and 
MPDC likewise advise the Municipal Development Council to identify the projects to be funded out of the lump sum appropriations, and to draw a resolution to reprogram the remaining appropriation totaling ₱698,308.20 to equally important projects which are allowed to be charged against the 20% development fund.
observations.managementAction: Most of the projects funded under continuing funds were already implemented during CY 2016. Funds were realigned and the remaining balance will be used as counterpart fund for the fund transfer from LGU/NGAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality encourage the barangay to establish materials recovery facility that will receive the mixed wastes for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill. Moreover, we suggested that the Municipal’s SWM Board strictly implement and monitor the collection and segregation of wastes at the barangay level as required by the provisions of RA No. 9003 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MENRO continuously encouraged the barangays to establish materials recovery facility in their area.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to monitor the proper segregation of wastes from source barangays. Lack of cooperation with the constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
Local School Board prepare an implementation and financial plan for the SEF budget which are in lump sum appropriation by indicating therein the itemized expenses and proper classification of expenses to be incurred;
Municipal Budget Officer limits the charging of expenses  to lump sum appropriation only to those expenses that are included in the financial plan; and
Municipal Accountant record the cost of expenses incurred in connection with the government operations/projects in their appropriate expense accounts to avoid misclassification of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made adjustments on the book to record the expenses in their appropriate expense accounts.
observations.reason: There were expenses incurred in CY 2016 that are still recorded in Other MOOE instead of appropriate expense accounts because of its inclusion in the CY 2016 budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
Concerned officials and employees to attach the official receipt for the refunded excess cash advance in the liquidation of their cash advances and submit the original copy of Official Receipts of their refunds to the Accounting Department and thereafter forward the same to COA for audit purposes; and
Municipal Accountant require complete documentation before recording the liquidation of cash advances and strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assigned a personnel to monitor the cash advances.
observations.reason: The in-charge accounting staff to monitor the cash advance is still gathering the proof of refund of the excess CAs for submission to COA.
